questions.stem: An enzyme works best at pH 7 but loses activity at pH 2. The most likely reason is that low pH:

choices.label: B
choices.text: Adds new amino acids to the enzyme

choices.label: C
choices.text: Alters charges and disrupts the active site's shape

questions.explanation: Extreme pH changes protonation of side chains, disrupting ionic and hydrogen bonds that maintain the active site, which lowers activity.

hints.prompt: An enzyme's catalytic activity depends most directly on which property of the protein?

choices.label: C

choices.label: D

hints.prompt: A drop to pH 2 floods the solution with H+ ions. What is the most direct effect on the enzyme's amino acid side chains?

hints.prompt: Two of the main options can be ruled out immediately as biochemically impossible for a change in pH. Which pair is that?
